svg.text: Scientific Hypothesis Agent for Novel Discovery — System Architecture

foreignObject.xhtml:div: SHANDY is an autonomous scientific discovery platform that orchestrates AI agents to analyze research data. It iteratively explores datasets, formulates and tests hypotheses through statistical analysis and PubMed literature review, generates visualizations, and produces comprehensive research reports with full provenance. Core features: OAuth authentication ("Sign in with GitHub/Google"-style secure login), API keys for programmatic automation, job sharing with granular read/edit permissions, and cost tracking across LLM providers. New capabilities: Job Chat for interactive Q&A with agentic code execution, ntfy.sh push notifications for real-time job alerts on any device, an extensible skills system for agent capabilities, and MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools. Security: Per-job sibling containers via Docker socket provide isolated execution environments; PostgreSQL row-level security ensures each user sees only their own data.

xhtml:div.style: font-family: 'Segoe UI', system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 9.5px; color: #475569; line-height: 1.45; text-align: justify;
xhtml:div.xhtml:p: set_current_user(session, user_id) sets app.current_user_id session variable. PostgreSQL evaluates RLS policies per-row using this variable. The bypass_rls() context manager allows admin and migration operations to skip policy checks.

g.text: DB ⇄ Filesystem dual sync via KnowledgeState

Packages apps into isolated, portable containers.

Database feature — users see only their data.
